hero.title: The Alters Last Variable Wiki
hero.description: Follow Jan Scientist into a new survival story featuring an underground base, four specialist Alters, nine terraforming arenas, cryosleep cycles, advanced research and the mystery of the Oasis.

gameFeature.title: The Alters Last Variable Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Build an underground base, manage four specialist Alters, terraform nine hostile arenas, and unravel the mystery of the Oasis in this sci-fi survival DLC by 11 bit studios.

tools.title: The Alters Last Variable
tools.subtitle: Everything you need to survive The Alters: Last Variable — release info, DLC setup, walkthroughs, specialist Alters, base building, terraforming and endings.

releaseDatePlatformsPrice.title: The Alters Last Variable Release Date, Price and Platforms
releaseDatePlatformsPrice.intro: Last Variable launched on July 13, 2026, as a paid expansion for The Alters. Compare its price, platform availability, ownership requirements, and store-specific features.

platforms.features: Steam achievements, Steam Cloud, Family Sharing and controller support
DRM-free offline installer with optional GOG Galaxy features
Offline single-player play, DualSense vibration and accessibility options
Also included with eligible Deluxe Edition and Deluxe Edition Upgrade purchases

platforms.availability: PC, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S
platforms.features: Approximately 20 hours of new campaign content

dlcAccessStoryPrerequisites.title: The Alters Last Variable DLC Access and Installation
dlcAccessStoryPrerequisites.intro: The expansion runs as a separate campaign and does not require an existing completed save. Follow these steps to confirm ownership, install the DLC, and start its story.

steps.title: Own The Alters Base Game
steps.description: Purchase or install the original The Alters on the same storefront and account used for the Last Variable expansion. The DLC cannot run as a standalone game.

steps.title: Purchase or Verify Last Variable
steps.description: Buy the expansion separately or confirm that it is included with your eligible Deluxe Edition content. Check the platform's add-on management page if the DLC is not automatically recognized.

steps.title: Install the Latest Game Update
steps.description: Update The Alters before launching it. On consoles, open the game's add-on management screen and confirm that Last Variable is installed rather than only owned.

steps.title: Open the New Game Menu
steps.description: Launch The Alters, choose Start New Game, and select the Last Variable campaign. Loading a completed base-game save is not required.

steps.title: Begin with a Separate Campaign State
steps.description: The expansion creates its own progression route, research sequence, objectives, resources, and save files. Base-game structures and inventory are not carried into the new story.

steps.title: Review the Story Prerequisite
steps.description: Last Variable follows the outcome in which Jan Scientist remains behind on the planet. Complete the original campaign first to understand the relationships, ethical choices, and events referenced by the expansion.

steps.title: Check Missing-DLC Problems
steps.description: Restart the storefront client, verify the game's installed files, confirm that the base game and DLC use the same regional account, and reinstall the add-on if Last Variable does not appear in the menu.

beginnerGuide.title: The Alters Last Variable Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: The opening cycle is controlled by a 14-day Cryosleep Chamber deadline. Prioritize essential repairs, resource outposts, the branching procedure, and your first Alter before spending materials on optional modules or unnecessary crafting.

steps.title: Complete Famous Last Words
objectives: Speak with Old Scientist and complete his final request.
Bury him and return to JanBot.
Inspect the damaged Research Lab and the locked Quantum Computer.
steps.keyCosts: Keep at least 10 Metals available for a Repair Kit.
steps.avoid: Do not spend the starting Metals on optional equipment before inspecting the damaged facilities.

steps.title: Start New Home and Forced Dormancy
objectives: Review the new base objectives after speaking with JanBot.
Track the 14-day deadline for constructing the Cryosleep Chamber.
Reserve resources for the chamber while expanding production.
steps.avoid: Do not treat the deadline as optional; delaying core research can leave too little time for construction.

steps.title: Establish the First Metals Outpost
objectives: Travel south from the base and locate the nearby Metals deposit.
Build a Metals Outpost and connect it to the base network.
Collect enough Metals for repairs, pylons, and required modules.
steps.avoid: Do not rely only on manually collected surface resources; automated Metals production is needed for the opening build order.

steps.title: Repair the Research Lab
objectives: Use the Repair Kit on the damaged Research Lab.
Inspect the laboratory whiteboard.
Speak with JanBot to unlock the route toward the Oasis.
steps.keyCosts: New Branching Procedure research uses 20 Rapidium and takes 2 working hours.
steps.avoid: Do not end the day before starting critical research when enough work time remains.

steps.title: Create the First Alter
objectives: Collect Rapidium from the deposit south of the Metals Outpost.
Complete New Branching Procedure.
Choose the first Alter according to the resource bottleneck you want to solve.
alterOptions: Physicist: 50% faster base maintenance and Workshop crafting.
Biologist: 100% faster Greenhouse work and eight-times faster Infirmary healing.
Chemist: 50% faster refining and faster cooking.
steps.avoid: Do not choose only by personality; match the Alter's production bonus to the facilities you plan to use most.

steps.title: Investigate the Oasis
objectives: Enter the Oasis and collect all three required samples.
Return before exhausting the day's exploration window.
Start Analysis of Samples in the Research Lab.
steps.keyCosts: Analysis of Samples takes 4 working hours.
steps.avoid: Do not leave the Oasis after collecting only one or two samples, because a second trip consumes another large block of the day.

steps.title: Build the Sample Analysis Module
objectives: Secure Metals, Rapidium, and Organics.
Add the Sample Analysis Module to the base.
Continue the Oasis research chain without interrupting essential resource production.
steps.keyCosts: Sample Analysis Module: 60 Metals, 50 Rapidium and 60 Organics.
steps.avoid: Do not begin construction until the Cryosleep Chamber reserve and basic production network are protected.

steps.title: Stabilize the Base Economy
objectives: Build a Dormitory for the growing crew.
Establish an Organics Outpost behind the base.
Craft Drill Cartridges when blocked deposits must be opened.
Extend pylons toward the Oasis Rapidium deposit.
Add a Refinery only when its input materials and staffing are ready.
steps.avoid: Avoid building multiple unstaffed facilities, overproducing food, or assigning Jan and every Alter to long projects at the same time.

walkthroughQuestGuide.title: The Alters Last Variable Walkthrough and Quest Guide
walkthroughQuestGuide.intro: The campaign progresses through repeating exploration, research, production, Field Laboratory, Terraformer, and cryosleep phases. Complete urgent construction before the end of each cycle, then use remaining hours for Alter requests, anomalies, upgrades, and additional resource reserves.

mainQuests: Find a Way into the Oasis
requiredActions: Complete Old Scientist's final request and speak with JanBot.
Inspect and repair the Research Lab.
Build the first Metals Outpost.
Research New Branching Procedure and create the first Alter.
Collect three Oasis samples and complete Analysis of Samples.
Build the Sample Analysis Module and Cryosleep Chamber.
importantCosts: Cryosleep Chamber: 50 Metals and 65 Rapidium.
Sample Analysis Module: 60 Metals, 50 Rapidium and 60 Organics.
First Terraformer: 30 Metals, 50 Rapidium and 50 Phased Rapidium.
Cryosleep preparation: 50 Organics for each person remaining inside the base.
optionalObjectives: Can't Do It Without Help for the Physicist.
Build the Infirmary before the Geologist's human test.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Install the Terraformer in Rocky Valley, complete urgent Alter requests, stock Organics, and enter cryosleep before the cycle expires.

requiredActions: Restore resource structures dismantled during the transition.
Research a method for passing the root barriers.
Explore the reopened Oasis routes.
Construct the first Field Laboratory on the second tier near the Metals Outpost.
Assign an Alter to the Field Laboratory on the final research day.
importantCosts: Field Laboratory: 50 Metals, 50 Minerals and 100 Organics.
Field Laboratory construction time: 2 working hours.
Field Laboratory research: 10 hours for most workers or approximately 6 hours 15 minutes for the Chemist.
optionalObjectives: Rebuild the resource network before expanding into optional modules.
Prepare enough Organics for every person who will remain in the base during cryosleep.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Finish the Field Laboratory assignment, collect all available resources, and enter cryosleep after the cycle's investigation is complete.

requiredActions: Visit the Field Laboratory and speak with JanBot to unlock Proxilium.
Build a Proxilium Tank and expand production.
Construct the Splitter needed for advanced materials.
Research and install a Tier II Terraformer in Misty Gorge.
Upgrade the Sample Analysis Module with a Magnetometer.
Complete Magnetometric Analysis.
importantCosts: Proxilium Tank: 150 Metals and 40 Minerals.
Splitter: 300 Metals, 150 Minerals and 100 Organics.
Tier II Terraformer: 60 Metals, 100 Rapidium and 100 Proxilium.
Magnetometer: 30 Metals and 30 Minerals.
Magnetometric Analysis: 12 working hours.
optionalObjectives: Waves of Our Memory for the Chemist.
Doctor Janhattan and the Rematerializer project.
Project Rehabilitation and Uses for Slime for the Biologist.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Terraform Misty Gorge, complete the sample analysis, and resolve the current Alter objectives before cryosleep.

requiredActions: Follow the new energy and radiation investigation.
Complete the Radiation Crystallization experiment.
Reach the raised central area of Misty Gorge.
Construct the second Field Laboratory.
Assign an Alter to complete the Field Laboratory work before the cycle ends.
importantCosts: Radiation Crystallization experiment: 12 working hours.
Reserve construction resources before beginning optional facility upgrades.
optionalObjectives: Finish unresolved personal quests from earlier cycles.
Upgrade production capacity for the advanced resources required later.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Complete the second Field Laboratory assignment and enter cryosleep with a reserve of advanced materials.

cycles.section: Advanced Cycles: Megafluid and Tier III Terraforming
requiredActions: Collect data from both Field Laboratories to unlock Megafluid.
Produce the materials required for two Tier III Terraformers.
Install Terraformers in Far Hill and Waterfall.
Upgrade the Sample Analysis Module with a Mass Spectrometer.
Complete Mass Spectrometric Analysis.
Build the third Field Laboratory in Far Hill.
importantCosts: Each Tier III Terraformer: 90 Metals, 200 Rapidium and 75 Megafluid.
Mass Spectrometer: 50 Metals and 50 Minerals.
Mass Spectrometric Analysis: 12 working hours.
optionalObjectives: Find the pink suitcase containing Scientist Jan's journal.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Activate both Terraformers, complete the third Field Laboratory assignment, and finish the Colossal Energy investigation.

requiredActions: Build the Resistor Blocks required for the final energy system.
Complete the planetary core analysis.
Research and craft the ENC Generator Alternator.
Assemble the generator and install it in Rocky Valley.
Activate the generator and return to the base.
Inspect the laboratory board, construct the additional Elevator Section, and descend.
importantCosts: Resistor Blocks: 50 Metals, 300 Phased Rapidium, 200 Proxilium and 100 Megafluid.
Resistor Blocks construction time: 4 working hours.
Core Analysis: 12 working hours.
Alternator research: 100 Phased Rapidium, 100 Proxilium and 100 Megafluid.
Alternator crafting: 50 Metals, 50 Organics and 160 Special Resources.
Generator assembly: 4 working hours.
optionalObjectives: Complete all remaining Alter conversations before activating the final sequence.
Create a manual save before committing to the final decision.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Enter the final area and choose how Jan responds to the planet's last variable.

requiredActions: Review the final discovery and select one of the two concluding paths.
Finish the ending sequence and credits.
Reload the latest autosave to experience the alternate conclusion.
optionalObjectives: Complete missing Alter storylines before repeating the finale.
Use earlier saves to test different production routes and first-Alter choices.
cycles.completionCheckpoint: Both final choices can be viewed without replaying the entire campaign by using the autosave created near the ending.

specialistAlters.title: The Alters Last Variable Best Specialist Alters
specialistAlters.intro: Jan Geologist is the strongest first creation because early Metal income and geology research control almost every other upgrade. Jan Physicist is the best second pick for Workshop output, maintenance, and processing technology; Jan Chemist and Jan Biologist become increasingly valuable once Field Labs, rare-resource chains, terraforming, the Greenhouse, and crew recovery enter the schedule.

specialists.scientificSpecialty: Geology, deposits, terrain barriers, anomalies, and advanced resource extraction.
researchContribution: Excels at root-barrier drilling, Magnetometer, Tier III Terraformer, Anomaly Repulsor, and late Oasis-device research.
Provides the strongest early boost to the Metal economy.
specialists.workEfficiency: 50% efficiency when mining Metal, making him the most useful opening-cycle resource specialist.
bestAssignments: Metal extraction during the opening cycle
Surface expeditions that combine deposits, samples, and story objectives
specialists.personality: Enthusiastic, adventurous, and eager to test bold ideas. He responds best when Jan takes his discoveries seriously and supports controlled experimentation.
specialists.fieldLabTiming: Keep him at the base until the main extraction upgrades, root access, anomaly protection, and his personal storyline are complete.
specialists.relationshipRule: Support his curiosity, repair experiment damage quickly, and give him rest after long mining or surface shifts.

specialists.scientificSpecialty: Physics, advanced instruments, resource-processing networks, equipment, and base systems.
researchContribution: Accelerates Splitter research from six hours to four hours and forty-five minutes.
Specializes in Tier II Terraformer, Polygonal Scanner, Rematerializer, and Mass Spectrometer projects.
Makes the transition from simple extraction to multi-input production chains much smoother.
specialists.workEfficiency: 50% efficiency in base maintenance and 50% efficiency when crafting Workshop items.
bestAssignments: Workshop queues and Repair Kit production
Late-day crafting while other Alters gather resources
specialists.personality: Highly self-assured and status-conscious, with a strong need to feel that his work is important.
specialists.fieldLabTiming: Send him only after the Splitter and essential instrument research are finished and the base has enough Repair Kits and spare parts.
specialists.relationshipRule: Recognize his expertise, avoid dismissive dialogue, and balance his workload with Recreation or personal conversations.

specialists.scientificSpecialty: Chemistry, refined materials, food production, sample work, and Field Lab technology.
researchContribution: Completes Field Laboratory research in six hours and fifteen minutes instead of the standard ten hours.
Supports chemical processing, sample analysis, anomaly-countermeasure work, and rare-resource production.
Becomes especially useful when Phased Rapidium, Proxylium, and Megafluid chains begin overlapping.
specialists.workEfficiency: 50% efficiency when refining Enriched Metal into Metal and Organics; he also prepares food faster.
bestAssignments: Sample experiments and chemical story projects
specialists.personality: Precise, emotionally restrained, and pragmatic. He values clear facts, efficient plans, and direct answers.
specialists.fieldLabTiming: Create him before the first Field Lab research bottleneck, then keep him at the base until key chemical projects and his storyline are resolved.
specialists.relationshipRule: Use practical dialogue, keep promises, and avoid assigning him to low-value work while major Laboratory projects are waiting.

specialists.scientificSpecialty: Biology, living samples, crop growth, medical recovery, slime research, and ecosystem response.
researchContribution: Accelerates Terraformer research and the Uses for Slime project.
Strengthens biological sample work and long-cycle survival planning.
Supports the research path connecting the Oasis, plant life, radiation recovery, and transformed regions.
specialists.workEfficiency: 100% efficiency when growing Greenhouse crops and eight-times-faster healing work in the Infirmary.
specialists.personality: Idealistic but worn down by past failures. He needs encouragement and visible proof that his work can still help the crew.
specialists.fieldLabTiming: Keep him available while injuries, food supply, Greenhouse output, or biological research remain unstable.
specialists.relationshipRule: Encourage his work, complete plant-related requests, and avoid sending him away while the base still depends on his healing or crop bonus.

undergroundBaseCryosleep.title: The Alters Last Variable Underground Base and Cryosleep
undergroundBaseCryosleep.intro: The underground base does not roll away from danger. Each cool phase is a preparation window before a thirteen-year hot phase forces the remaining crew into cryosleep. Build the Cryosleep Chamber within its fourteen-day objective, protect critical production and life-support modules, and treat the final evening as a hard logistics deadline rather than another normal work shift.

steps.title: Restore the Research Core
requirements: Build one Repair Kit in the Workshop for 10 Metal.
Repair the damaged Research Lab.
Research New Branching Procedure to unlock the specialist crew path.
steps.baseRule: Do not spend the opening Metal reserve on comfort modules before the Laboratory and basic extraction are operational.
steps.failurePrevented: A delayed Research Lab blocks Alter creation, Cryosleep preparation, Terraformer work, and the entire technology chain.

steps.title: Build the Cryosleep Chamber
steps.timing: Complete the Forced Dormancy objective within 14 days
requirements: 50 Metal
65 Rapidium
A valid underground placement cell connected to the working base
steps.baseRule: Place the chamber early enough that its footprint does not cut off future corridors, large modules, or expansion routes through the varied underground soil.
steps.failurePrevented: Without the chamber, the crew cannot safely pass the planet's years-long hot phase.

steps.title: Plan Around Soil and Module Footprints
requirements: Use the placement preview to identify buildable soil.
Reserve connected space for Laboratory, Workshop, storage, sleeping, food, medical, morale, and power-support functions.
Keep production modules close to storage and Workshop routes while separating rest areas from constant emergency work.
steps.baseRule: Build the mandatory chain first, then fill remaining cells with comfort modules. A compact layout reduces walking time and leaves fewer critical rooms isolated by awkward soil.
steps.failurePrevented: Poor placement can consume the only practical cells for later modules and force expensive rebuilding during a short cooldown.

steps.title: Create a Critical-Power and Repair Buffer
requirements: Keep life support, Laboratory, Workshop, storage, and essential processing online before optional comfort systems.
Stock Repair Kits and Metal for earthquake damage.
Maintain Radiation Filters, batteries, food, medicine, and spare construction materials.
steps.baseRule: Do not run every new processor at once if the base cannot also support repairs, meals, sleep, and medical recovery.
steps.failurePrevented: Earthquakes, radiation waves, and overloaded production can turn one damaged module into a chain of stalled research and exhausted Alters.

steps.title: Reserve Cryosleep Organics
steps.timing: Before the last day of every cool phase
requirements: 50 Organics for each Jan entering cryosleep.
100 Organics for Jan Scientist plus one Alter.
150 Organics for Jan Scientist plus two Alters.
steps.baseRule: Treat this Organics reserve as untouchable. Field Lab assignees remain outside the base and do not add to the chamber's current occupant cost.
steps.failurePrevented: Spending the reserve on food, modules, or experiments can leave the crew unable to end the cycle safely.

steps.title: Run the Final-Day Checklist
steps.timing: Evening or night of the final day
requirements: Finish urgent research and personal conversations.
Heal injuries, lower radiation, feed the crew, and restore sleep and morale.
Assign the selected Alter to a Field Lab only after his important base work is complete.
Dismantle any surface structures named in the cycle warning so their parts return to storage.
Enter cryosleep after all final shifts and collection runs are complete.
steps.baseRule: Do not sleep early unless the surface is already exhausted; the final day is valuable for stockpiling, samples, and relationship events.
steps.failurePrevented: Low readiness can trigger an Alter Revolt, while forgotten surface hardware and unfinished conversations create shortages or missable story progress after waking.

steps.title: Rebuild in the Correct Wake-Up Order
requirements: Restore the Organics Outpost first.
Replace dismantled pipelines, Phasers, Extractors, Outposts, and support structures.
Inspect base damage and crew readiness before assigning full shifts.
Visit active Field Labs and collect new research progress.
Use the short cooldown to explore newly transformed terrain and collect samples.
steps.baseRule: Restore survival income before rebuilding optional production branches.
steps.failurePrevented: Reconnecting rare-resource chains before food and Organics can create an impressive factory with no safe path to the next cryosleep.

terraformingOasisResearch.title: The Alters Last Variable Terraforming and Research Arenas
terraformingOasisResearch.intro: The map contains nine connected arenas: the Oasis plus eight regions transformed through three Terraformer tiers. Every transformation changes routes, exposes new construction sites and pickables, and advances the Phased Rapidium, Proxylium, and Megafluid research chain. Planetary activity rises as the project expands, so the safest route is to transform one research stage at a time, rebuild protection, and explore immediately during the next cooldown.

arenas.surfaceRole: The protected living ecosystem at the center of Jan Scientist's investigation.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Initial Rapidium access, the first biological evidence, and the Sample Analysis Module research path.
arenas.researchPriority: Collect all three opening samples, repair the Laboratory, and build the Sample Analysis Module before expanding the experiment tree.
arenas.hazardResponse: Use the Oasis as the comparison point for every transformed region; do not spend the early cooldown only mining.

arenas.surfaceRole: First full terraforming test and the location of the first Field Lab site.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Metal access, root-barrier progression, Field Lab deployment, and early Phased Rapidium infrastructure.
arenas.researchPriority: Transform first, finish the main Metal upgrades, then place the first Field Lab only after choosing which specialist can leave base work.
arenas.hazardResponse: Expect newly opened paths and stronger planetary reactions; carry protection charges and combine every trip with sample collection.

arenas.surfaceRole: Central logistics arena linking the underground base to Organics, processing equipment, and multiple regional routes.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: New building space, processing connections, Organics access, and technology experiments based on recovered debris.
arenas.researchPriority: Keep the Organics route reliable, recover all debris, and leave clear pipeline corridors for later multi-resource production.
arenas.hazardResponse: Rebuild this region first after cryosleep because failures here can disconnect several other arenas at once.

arenas.surfaceRole: Early expansion zone with Enriched Metal and a compact set of mixed samples.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Enriched Metal supply and additional data for extraction, outpost, and radiation-support experiments.
arenas.researchPriority: Secure the Enriched Metal Outpost, then clear caves and dead ends before the next hot phase.
arenas.hazardResponse: Short routes make it suitable for a late-day sweep, but narrow passages punish low battery or radiation protection.

arenas.surfaceRole: Midgame research hub and location of the second Field Lab.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Second Field Lab access, advanced sample experiments, and a key junction for later Phased Rapidium and Megafluid pipelines.
arenas.researchPriority: Terraform before assigning a second Alter, collect the upper-path samples, and preserve a clean route for Splitters and pipelines.
arenas.hazardResponse: Roots and vertical paths increase travel time; enter with upgraded climbing, battery capacity, and a defined sample route.

arenas.surfaceRole: High-value mineral and processing region used by the final rare-resource network.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Mineral extraction and a transformed input required for the Megafluid production chain.
arenas.researchPriority: Collect the dense Obsidian set, establish the Mineral line, and prepare Phaser and Splitter connections before rebuilding the final network.
arenas.hazardResponse: Use strong radiation protection and finish the region in one planned circuit; repeated partial trips waste the short cooldown.

arenas.surfaceRole: Ruined research zone containing technology debris, story objects, and a mineral input for rare-resource production.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Proxylium-related infrastructure, Chemist story progress, and additional permanent experiment bonuses.
arenas.researchPriority: Search the ruined base and long upper route before leaving; several objectives can be cleared in the same expedition.
arenas.hazardResponse: Earthquake and radiation pressure increase by this stage, so avoid entering without filters, healing supplies, and enough time to return.

arenas.surfaceRole: Late-game biological and geological sample zone opened after advanced root and terraforming research.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Late Sample Analysis Module experiments and final evidence connecting transformed land to Oasis survival.
arenas.researchPriority: Use drilling upgrades to open the route, collect the entire mixed sample set, and complete remaining permanent bonuses.
arenas.hazardResponse: Treat it as a single high-priority cooldown expedition because access is late and the planet's defense is already severe.

arenas.surfaceRole: Final high-tier region and location of the third Field Lab.
arenas.resourceOrUnlock: Third Field Lab access, final research data, and the last major step toward the ENC Generator sequence.
arenas.researchPriority: Terraform, reach the highest level, place the third Field Lab, collect every remaining sample, and finish specialist storylines before activating the final device.
arenas.hazardResponse: Bring maximum practical radiation protection and leave enough time for the long vertical return route.

endingsChoicesAchievements.title: The Alters Last Variable Endings and Achievements
endingsChoicesAchievements.intro: Last Variable follows the base-game branch in which Jan Scientist remains on the planet to study the Oasis. Most ordinary dialogue choices shape conversations rather than the ending; the decisive branch comes in the final exchange with JanBot. Field Lab assignments, specialist aging, unfinished personal stories, and the timing of the ENC Generator still change what the final journey feels like and determine whether completion objectives remain available.

items.heading: Story Prerequisite: The Scientist Stayed
items.summary: The expansion begins from the base-game ending where Jan Scientist remains behind while the other Jans leave the planet.
details: The DLC is launched as its own story route and does not require the player's original ending save.
The old research effort, underground base, Oasis mystery, and JanBot form the starting point.
Playing the base game first provides the full context for Jan Scientist's choice and obsession.

items.heading: Field Lab Choices and Specialist Fate
items.summary: Each Field Lab assignment removes one specialist from normal base work and exposes him to the passage of years outside Jan Scientist's cryosleep.
details: The first Field Lab is reached through Rocky Valley, the second through Misty Gorge, and the third through Far Hill.
Finish the selected specialist's urgent research, useful production work, and personal conversations before assignment.
Assigned Alters continue the Oasis project across cycles and visibly age while Jan Scientist sleeps.
The same specialist type can be created again later, which also unlocks the Wait, you can do that? achievement.

items.heading: Ending One: Create a Successor
items.summary: Jan accepts a continuing succession of researchers so the Oasis project can outlive the current crew.
requirements: Reach the final JanBot conversation after the ENC Generator sequence.
Choose Create a Successor.
items.outcome: Jan Scientist's legacy becomes a self-perpetuating research program: a new Alter and JanBot continue the work instead of allowing the unanswered question to end with one lifetime.
items.theme: Knowledge, continuity, and obsession are placed above closure.

items.heading: Ending Two: Break the Cycle
items.summary: Jan rejects the succession model and chooses to end the endless research cycle.
requirements: Reach the final JanBot conversation after the ENC Generator sequence.
Choose Break the Cycle.
items.outcome: Jan Scientist refuses to make another life carry the same burden and accepts that the project must stop, even if the Oasis cannot be reduced to a final complete answer.
items.theme: Mortality, restraint, and acceptance are placed above unlimited discovery.

items.heading: What the Oasis Means at the End
items.summary: The Oasis becomes a test of whether scientific curiosity justifies an endless chain of created lives.
details: Terraforming produces knowledge but also provokes stronger earthquakes, radiation waves, and defensive planetary reactions.
The final choice reframes the Oasis from a simple puzzle into an ethical boundary.
Create a Successor treats the mystery as important enough to continue indefinitely.
Break the Cycle treats acceptance and responsibility as more important than total explanation.

items.heading: Best Save Points and Missable Objectives
items.summary: Use three practical checkpoints to protect storylines and view both endings efficiently.
savePoints: Before permanently assigning each Alter to a Field Lab: finish his available conversations and specialist projects.
Before activating the ENC Generator: complete remaining personal incidents, experiments, sample collection, and base preparation.
At the final JanBot conversation: the completion autosave returns to this branch, allowing both endings without replaying the full expansion.
missables: Complete all four Scientist-Alter storylines before the final sequence for Caring after all.
Respond to new conversation icons after each cryosleep and major research step.
Collect region samples and finish optional Sample Analysis Module experiments before ending the project.
Create a specialist type for a second time before completion for Wait, you can do that?.

items.heading: Last Variable Achievement and Trophy Checklist
items.summary: The expansion adds seven achievements worth 210 Gamerscore on Xbox and seven trophies on PlayStation.

achievements.name: Wait, you can do that?
achievements.requirement: Create the same Alter for a second time.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Alters: Last Variable

questions.question: Do I need to own The Alters to play The Alters: Last Variable?
questions.answer: Yes. The Alters: Last Variable is a paid story DLC, not a standalone game. You must own and install the original The Alters on the same storefront and account before the expansion can launch.

questions.question: How long is The Alters: Last Variable and how much does it cost?
questions.answer: The Alters: Last Variable offers roughly 20 hours of new campaign content and costs $14.99 on Steam, Epic Games Store, GOG, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

questions.question: Which platforms is The Alters: Last Variable available on?
questions.answer: The Alters: Last Variable launched on July 13, 2026 for Windows PC (Steam, Epic and GOG), PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, and is also included with eligible Deluxe Edition purchases.

questions.question: Do I need a completed save to start The Alters: Last Variable?
questions.answer: No. The Alters: Last Variable runs as its own separate campaign and does not require your original ending save. However, playing the base game first gives full context for why Jan Scientist remains on the planet.

questions.question: Who are the four specialist Alters in The Alters: Last Variable?
questions.answer: The Alters: Last Variable lets you create Jan Geologist, Jan Physicist, Jan Chemist and Jan Biologist. Jan Geologist is the strongest first pick because early Metal income and geology research unlock almost every other upgrade.

questions.question: How many endings does The Alters: Last Variable have?
questions.answer: The Alters: Last Variable has two conclusions reached through the final JanBot conversation: Create a Successor and Break the Cycle. A completion autosave near the ending lets you view both without replaying the full expansion.
